annotation: Fable 5 (claude-fable-5), single pass, 2026-07-07; no second annotator yet (known limitation, recorded in the hypothesis note).

dev.scenario: Broadest classical Sanskrit→English reference that also marks Vedic accents.
A Sanskritized Pali-looking form in the Mahāvastu, absent from MW.
Deepest scholarly treatment with fullest citations; German acceptable.
Clean numbered senses and translated literary quotations for classical (non-Vedic) reading.
Latin glosses and Indo-European cognates by the founder of comparative grammar.
Identify a Sanskrit plant name botanically.
Administrative/fiscal term in an inscription, technical sense needed.
Identify and locate a proper name in the Mahābhārata.
The story and identity behind a Purāṇic name, in clear English.
Classical synonyms of a word, in the traditional kośa form.
A name or subject in Vedic literature, with scholarly references.
A root's grammatical profile — class, seṭ/aniṭ, pada, kṛt derivatives.
The earliest major Sanskrit–English dictionary, MW's foundation.
Compare Monier-Williams 1872 vs 1899 treatment of a word.
A compact, accented student's dictionary for reading.
A word/sense missing from PW; where are the scholarly additions?

test.scenario: You want the definition IN Sanskrit, with śāstric quotations — a Sanskrit–Sanskrit encyclopedic lexicon.
You met a Sanskrit work title or author name and want to know where the work is attested and catalogued.
MW marks a word 'L.' (lexicographers only) and you want to verify it in the indigenous lexica it came from.
A Ṝgvedic word: you want its accent and every verse location, grouped by construction/meaning.
A beginner reading a graded reader wants the reader's own vocabulary, not a full dictionary.
You read German and want a concise one-volume dictionary rather than the seven-volume PWG.
